Additional context from the user's mention:
<issue identifier="REN-119">
<title>TC-003: Implement text input widget</title>
<description>
## Goal

Implement a styled text input widget (`widget.TextInput`) that wraps `charm.land/bubbles/v2/textinput`, supports a placeholder, accepts a validation callback, and renders an inline error message when validation fails. Used downstream for chat messages, issue creation, and search filters.

## Scope

Atomic single-concern widget. Files touched:

* `widget/textinput.go` — model, `New`, `Update`, `View`, `SetSize`, `Focus`, `Blur`, options, `Value`, `SetValue`, `Reset`, `Err`.
* `widget/textinput_test.go` — table-driven model + view tests.
* `widget/testdata/` (if needed) — `cupaloy` golden snapshots for rendered views.

No new dependencies. No changes outside `widget/` and `theme/` (only if a missing style needs to be added — see below).

## Public API (godoc required on every export)

```go
// ValidateFunc reports an error for an invalid input value, or nil if valid.
type ValidateFunc func(string) error

// Option configures a TextInput at construction time.
type Option func(*TextInput)

// WithPlaceholder sets the placeholder text shown when the input is empty.
func WithPlaceholder(s string) Option

// WithValidate sets the validation callback invoked on every change.
// The most recent error is exposed via Err and rendered below the input.
func WithValidate(fn ValidateFunc) Option

// WithCharLimit caps the maximum number of characters accepted.
func WithCharLimit(n int) Option

// WithWidth sets the display width in cells.
func WithWidth(n int) Option

// New constructs a TextInput. Apply options for placeholder, validation, etc.
func New(opts ...Option) TextInput

// TextInput is a styled wrapper around bubbles/v2/textinput that implements
// component.Component and surfaces a validation error below the field.
type TextInput struct { /* ... */ }

func (t TextInput) Init() tea.Cmd
func (t TextInput) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd)
func (t TextInput) View() string
func (t *TextInput) SetSize(width, height int)
func (t *TextInput) Focus()
func (t *TextInput) Blur()
func (t TextInput) Value() string
func (t *TextInput) SetValue(s string)
func (t *TextInput) Reset()
func (t TextInput) Err() error
```

Notes:

* Implements `component.Component` (`tea.Model` + `SetSize` + `Focus` + `Blur`).
* Accept Bubbles `textinput` options where it makes sense (placeholder, char limit, width) — do not re-export the upstream model directly; keep the surface stable.
* Validation runs inside `Update` after the underlying textinput processes the keypress, then the error is cached on the model.

## Architecture / Patterns ([CLAUDE.md](<http://CLAUDE.md>))

* Wraps `charm.land/bubbles/v2/textinput` — no parallel reimplementation of cursor/edit logic.
* Reads colors from `theme/` only; no hardcoded `lipgloss.Color` literals. Use existing styles where possible (`theme.Muted`, `theme.Dimmed`) and the palette (`theme.TextPrimary`, `theme.TextSecondary`, `theme.TextTertiary`, `theme.SurfaceBorder`). If a dedicated `ErrorText` style or palette entry is missing, add it to `theme/styles.go` / `theme/palette.go` in this same change — keep the addition minimal and documented.
* View layout: bordered field (`theme.CardBorder` or a slimmer variant) with the textinput rendered inside; if `Err() != nil` and the field has a value, render the error message on the line below in the error style. Hide the error when the field is empty so a fresh field doesn't scream "required" before the user types.
* Focus/Blur delegate to the embedded `textinput.Model`. The wrapper stores no extra focus state.
* `SetSize` sets the inner textinput width to `width - horizontal padding/border`. Height is single-line; ignore the height argument (document it).
* No package-level state. No goroutines. No `init()`.

## Error Handling

* `Err()` returns the last validation error or nil. Errors are domain values from the caller's `ValidateFunc` — do not wrap them.
* The widget itself returns no errors from `New` or `Update`; invalid options are a programmer error and may panic with a clear message only for impossible states (e.g., negative width). Per [CLAUDE.md](<http://CLAUDE.md>), prefer returning errors over panicking, but constructor options here are checked at compile time, so panicking on negative width is acceptable and aligned with Bubbles' own behavior.

## Logging

* `charmbracelet/log` to stderr only when something is genuinely surprising. A user-facing widget should be silent in the happy path. Do not log on every keypress or validation failure.

## Testing (target ≥80%, this widget should reach 90%+)

* stdlib `testing`, table-driven, no testify.
* Model tests: construct with each option, drive `Update` with synthetic `tea.KeyPressMsg` events, assert `Value()`, `Err()`, focus state, and char-limit truncation.
* View tests: golden files via `cupaloy` for representative states — empty+placeholder, focused+typed, blurred+typed, validation error visible, char-limit reached. Snapshots live under `widget/testdata/`.
* Cover `Reset()`, `SetValue()`, and the no-error-while-empty rule explicitly.

## Linting / Formatting

* `make fmt` (gofumpt) and `make lint` (golangci-lint v2: govet, staticcheck, gofumpt, errcheck, gosec, gocritic, revive) must pass clean.
* All exported identifiers carry godoc starting with the identifier name.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `widget.TextInput` exists with the API above and implements `component.Component`.
- [ ] Placeholder, validation callback, char limit, and width are all configurable via `Option`s.
- [ ] Error message renders below the field only when `Err() != nil` and value is non-empty.
- [ ] Colors come exclusively from `theme/`.
- [ ] `make build`, `make test -race`, `make lint`, `make vuln` all pass.
- [ ] Coverage for `widget/textinput.go` ≥ 90%; golden snapshots committed under `widget/testdata/`.
- [ ] Every exported symbol has a godoc comment.
- [ ] No new direct dependencies outside the Charm v2 stack already in `go.mod`.

## Out of Scope

* Multi-line editing — that belongs in a separate `textarea` widget (TC-00x, future).
* History, autocomplete, suggestions, masked/password mode — file follow-ups if requested; do not bundle here.
* Downstream integration (chat, issue creation, search filter screens) — those live in consumer repos.
</description>
<team name="Rensei"/>
<project name="tui-components"/>
</issue>
